# komodo Provider

Terraform provider for managing Komodo resources via the Komodo API.

## Authentication

The provider supports two authentication methods. Exactly one must be configured.

### Username / Password

```terraform
provider "komodo" {
  endpoint = "https://your-komodo-server.com"
  username = "your-username"
  password = "your-password"
}
```

Credentials can also be supplied via environment variables:

```shell
export KOMODO_ENDPOINT="https://your-komodo-server.com"
export KOMODO_USERNAME="your-username"
export KOMODO_PASSWORD="your-password"
```

### API Key / Secret

Suitable for service users or any account that has a `komodo_api_key` resource.

```terraform
provider "komodo" {
  endpoint   = "https://your-komodo-server.com"
  api_key    = "your-api-key"
  api_secret = "your-api-secret"
}
```

Credentials can also be supplied via environment variables:

```shell
export KOMODO_ENDPOINT="https://your-komodo-server.com"
export KOMODO_API_KEY="your-api-key"
export KOMODO_API_SECRET="your-api-secret"
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `api_key` (String) The Komodo API key. Can also be set via the KOMODO_API_KEY environment variable. Use with `api_secret` as an alternative to `username`/`password`.
- `api_secret` (String, Sensitive) The Komodo API secret. Can also be set via the KOMODO_API_SECRET environment variable. Use with `api_key` as an alternative to `username`/`password`.
- `endpoint` (String) The Komodo API endpoint URL. Can also be set via the KOMODO_ENDPOINT environment variable.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) The Komodo password. Can also be set via the KOMODO_PASSWORD environment variable.
- `username` (String) The Komodo username. Can also be set via the KOMODO_USERNAME environment variable.
